decision_point: In an adult with an isolated grade I-II medial collateral ligament injury of the knee, is functional bracing and rehabilitation or surgical repair preferred?
clinical_vignette:
Typical adult patient for whom this decision arises (no atypical comorbidities or contraindications).

Decision: In an adult with an isolated grade I-II medial collateral ligament injury of the knee, is functional bracing and rehabilitation or surgical repair preferred?
Options:
  1. Functional rehabilitation
  2. Surgical repair
label: settled
label_rationale: Isolated low-grade MCL injuries heal reliably without surgery; repair is reserved for high-grade or combined instabilities.
